title: quercus lusitanica subsp. cerrioides
intro: Quercus lusitanica subsp. cerrioides, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus lusitanica subsp. cerrioides are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves and bark are also used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and extracts.
